messages.content:
The Benefits of Playing Video Games
Isabela Granic, Adam Lobel, and Rutger C. M. E. Engels
Radboud University Nijmege, convert to apa citation
Granic, I., Lobel, A., & Engels, R. C. M. E. (2023). The benefits of playing video games. Radboud University Nijmegen.
Granic, I., Lobel, A., & Engels, R. C. M. E. (2023). The benefits of playing video games. American Psychologist. Retrieved from https://www.apa.org/pubs/journals/releases/amp-a0034857.pdf

I apologize for the oversight in my previous response. The correct APA citation for a document accessed online would look like this, based on the information given and assuming the year provided initially was accurate to the document's publication year. However, if the document was not actually published in 2023, please adjust the citation accordingly using the correct publication year:

Granic, I., Lobel, A., & Engels, R. C. M. E. (Year). The benefits of playing video games. American Psychologist. Retrieved from https://www.apa.org/pubs/journals/releases/amp-a0034857.pdf

Note: Replace "Year" with the correct publication year of the document.

Given the context you've provided, it appears there might have been a misunderstanding with the year. If the given URL and details refer to an existing publication, it might actually pertain to an article titled "The Benefits of Playing Video Games" that was published in 2014, not 2023. Here's how you would correctly cite it in APA format, including the 2014 publication year:

Granic, I., Lobel, A., & Engels, R. C. M. E. (2014). The benefits of playing video games. American Psychologist, 69(1), 66–78. https://doi.org/10.1037/a0034857

Text 1: authored by Paul J. C. Adachi, Teena Willoughby / from an article titled The
link between playing video games and positive youth outcomes / from a journal titled
Child Development Perspectives / Volume 11 / Issue 3 / 2021 / pp. 91-94 / DOI:
https://doi.org/10.1038/d41586-02 convert to apa format
Adachi, P. J. C., & Willoughby, T. (2021). The link between playing video games and positive youth outcomes. Child Development Perspectives, 11(3), 91-94. https://doi.org/10.1038/d41586-02

Text 2: authored by Wanda M. Williams, Cynthia G. Ayres, Brian Mulder / from a
journal article titled Can active video games improve physical activity in adolescents? /
from a journal titled International Journal of Public Health / Volume 17 / Issue 2 / 2020
/ pp. 3–6 / DOI: https://doi.org/10.1093 apa
Williams, W. M., Ayres, C. G., & Mulder, B. (2020). Can active video games improve physical activity in adolescents? International Journal of Public Health, 17(2), 3-6. https://doi.org/10.1093

Text 3: authored by Craig A. Anderson, Wayne Warburton / from a book chapter titled
The impact of violent video games / from a book titled Growing Up Fast and Furious:
The Impacts of Violent Media on Children / 2021 / Edited by D. Braunstein / The
Federation Press / pp.148-168

Anderson, C. A., & Warburton, W. (2021). The impact of violent video games. In D. Braunstein (Ed.), Growing Up Fast and Furious: The Impacts of Violent Media on Children (pp. 148-168). The Federation Press.

Text 4: authored by Christopher L. Groves / from a book chapter titled Negative effects
of video game play / from a book titled Handbook of Digital Games and Entertainment /
/ Edited by Ryohei Nakatsu / 2020 / Springer / pp. 86-98
Groves, C. L. (2020). Negative effects of video game play. In R. Nakatsu (Ed.), Handbook of Digital Games and Entertainment (pp. 86-98). Springer.

Prot, S., Anderson, C. A., Gentile, D. A., Brown, S. C., & Swing, E. L. (2014). The positive and negative effects of video game play. Media and the well-being of children and adolescents, 109, 2010-2014.

make this an in text citation

convert the below to in text citation Adachi, P. J. C., & Willoughby, T. (2021). The link between playing video games and positive youth outcomes. Child Development Perspectives, 11(3), 91-94. https://doi.org/10.1038/d41586-02

Anderson, C. A., & Warburton, W. (2021). The impact of violent video games. In D. Braunstein (Ed.), Growing Up Fast and Furious: The Impacts of Violent Media on Children (pp. 148-168). The Federation Press.

Granic, I., Lobel, A., & Engels, R. C. M. E. (2014). The benefits of playing video games. American Psychologist, 69(1), 66–78. https://doi.org/10.1037/a0034857

Groves, C. L. (2020). Negative effects of video game play. In R. Nakatsu (Ed.), Handbook of Digital Games and Entertainment (pp. 86-98). Springer.

Prot, S., Anderson, C. A., Gentile, D. A., Brown, S. C., & Swing, E. L. (2014). The positive and negative effects of video game play. Media and the well-being of children and adolescents, 109, 2010-2014.

Williams, W. M., Ayres, C. G., & Mulder, B. (2020). Can active video games improve physical activity in adolescents? International Journal of Public Health, 17(2), 3-6. https://doi.org/10.1093

(Adachi & Willoughby, 2021)

(Anderson & Warburton, 2021)

(Granic, Lobel, & Engels, 2014)

(Groves, 2020)

(Prot et al., 2014)

(Williams, Ayres, & Mulder, 2020)
